name: Hunab Ku
description: Hunab Ku, meaning "Only God" or "One God," represents the supreme creator deity in Mayan mythology. He is often considered to be beyond human comprehension, existing in a realm beyond duality and accessible only through spiritual transcendence. Hunab Ku is the source of all creation, the origin from which all other deities and the universe itself emanated. While not directly involved in the day-to-day affairs of the world like other Mayan gods, Hunab Ku represents the ultimate unity and potential for spiritual growth. He is the center of the Mayan cosmos, the source of energy and consciousness. Some interpretations suggest he is the force behind the cyclical nature of time and the universe's constant renewal. His influence is subtle but pervasive, representing the underlying principle of existence. He is sometimes symbolized by a square within a circle, representing the balance between the material world and the spiritual realm. While not typically depicted in Mayan art in a concrete anthropomorphic form, his presence is felt through the interconnectedness of all things and the pursuit of enlightenment. Hunab Ku is the unseen architect of reality, the ultimate source of life and the potential for transcendence. He is the ultimate abstract concept of divinity within the Mayan pantheon, a force more than a personality. He embodies the infinite possibilities of creation and the inherent unity of all existence.
subtitle: The One God, Source of All

keyMyths.title: The Breath of Creation
keyMyths.description: Hunab Ku breathed life into the void, initiating the creation of the universe and all that exists within it. This act established the foundation for the Mayan cosmos.

keyMyths.title: The Source of the Gods
keyMyths.description: All other Mayan deities are believed to have emanated from Hunab Ku, making him the ultimate progenitor. They are extensions of his divine essence.

keyMyths.title: The Center of the Galaxy
keyMyths.description: Hunab Ku is sometimes associated with the center of the Milky Way galaxy, representing a cosmic source of energy. He is the heart of the universe.

cultural.modernLegacy: Hunab Ku's influence is primarily found in New Age spiritual movements and Mayan cultural revival efforts. He symbolizes unity, cosmic consciousness, and the interconnectedness of all things, inspiring spiritual seekers and artists alike. Some modern interpretations associate Hunab Ku with the Galactic Center, viewing him as a source of cosmic energy and enlightenment.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Hunab Ku's origins are somewhat enigmatic within Mayan cosmology. He is often described as self-created or existing eternally, preceding all other deities and the universe itself. Unlike other Mayan gods who feature prominently in creation myths, Hunab Ku remains aloof and transcendent. He is not born from a union of other deities but rather emanates as the singular source of existence. Some scholars suggest that the concept of Hunab Ku gained prominence later in Mayan history, possibly influenced by interactions with other cultures. Regardless of its precise origins, Hunab Ku represents the ultimate abstraction of divinity, a unified and unknowable force behind all creation.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: While Hunab Ku is considered the supreme creator, he rarely appears directly in Mayan myths and stories. This is because he exists beyond the realm of human comprehension and interaction. He does not intervene in the affairs of humans or other deities. Instead, he is seen as the ultimate source from which all other deities and the universe emanate. These other deities, such as Itzamná and Kukulkan, take on the active roles in the creation and maintenance of the world. Therefore, stories about the creation of humans or the establishment of the cosmos do not usually feature Hunab Ku directly, but rather, he is understood as the underlying power behind these events.

extendedContent.title: Worship & Cult
extendedContent.content: Due to his abstract and transcendent nature, Hunab Ku was not typically the direct object of widespread worship or cult practices in the traditional Mayan religion. There are no known temples or specific rituals dedicated solely to Hunab Ku. Instead, Mayan religious practices focused on deities who were believed to be more directly involved in human affairs, such as gods of rain, maize, and fertility. However, the concept of Hunab Ku may have been invoked in more esoteric or philosophical contexts, perhaps by priests or spiritual leaders seeking a deeper understanding of the universe and its origins. The veneration of Hunab Ku was more of an acknowledgement of the ultimate source of existence rather than a direct plea for intervention or favor.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Hunab Ku's legacy extends beyond the confines of traditional Mayan religious practices. The concept of a single, supreme creator god has resonated with various spiritual and philosophical movements throughout history. In modern times, Hunab Ku has been embraced by some New Age spiritualists and Mayan cultural revivalists as a symbol of unity, cosmic consciousness, and the interconnectedness of all things. Some interpretations associate Hunab Ku with the Galactic Center, viewing him as a source of cosmic energy and enlightenment. While these interpretations may differ from the original Mayan understanding, they demonstrate the enduring appeal of the idea of a single, unified source of creation.

shortDescription: Hunab Ku is the supreme creator deity in Mayan mythology, representing the source of all creation and existing beyond human comprehension.
